articles.title: 2 Stocks That Should be on Your Radar as the Iran War Shifts Global Energy Markets
articles.expand: Key PointsRoughly 20% of global oil and LNG supplies remain disrupted due to the Strait of Hormuz closure.

articles.title: USD: Data-heavy week shapes Fed view – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities economists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir anticipate a normalization in United States (US) labor data, with nonfarm payrolls at 80k, unemployment at 4.3% and modest wage growth.

articles.title: EUR/CHF: Short-term bounce, longer-term decline – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister expects EUR/CHF to rise in coming months as the Euro (EUR) recovers and markets push back Swiss National Bank (SNB) rate hikes, with the bank’s strategy combining verbal intervention and steady rates.

articles.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent: US is opening up Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: In an interview with Fox News on Monday, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the US is opening up the Strait of Hormuz and added that they have "absolute control" of it, per Reuters.

articles.title: NZD: RBNZ pushes back on supply-shock hikes – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage highlights comments from Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) policy member Prasanna Gai, who argues the Strait of Hormuz supply shock does not warrant automatic rate hikes.

articles.title: Bessent: The world is in an 8-10 million barrel oil supply deficit
articles.expand: I would be interested to see the math on Bessent's calculation on how many barrels are missing. The outlines of it are usually that 20 mbpd goes through the Strait of Hormuz daily under normal conditions. Now that's down to virtually zero, however Saudi Arabia fired up an east-west pipeline to the Red Sea and other mitigation efforts via land have ...

articles.title: EUR/USD: Dollar seen weakening versus Euro – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas economists expect the United States (US) economy to grow above potential in 2026, with Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at 2.4% and inflation at 3.3%, while the Fed Funds target range is seen steady at 3.5%-3.75%.

articles.title: Debunked Strike, Real War Risk: Dollar Rallies on Panic Hedge as Hormuz Tensions Rise
articles.expand: Markets were whipsawed today by a dramatic but ultimately false headline—and the reaction says everything about current risk conditions. Reports that Iranian missiles had struck a US Navy vessel near the Strait of Hormuz sent oil surging and triggered an immediate rush into the Dollar as a panic hedge. The logic was straightforward. articles.title: Euro area: Weak confidence clouds activity outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists note that firm and consumer confidence fell more than expected in April, with Commission indices at multi‑year lows. Despite slightly positive 1Q Gross Domestic Product (GDP), domestic demand appears subdued and credit conditions are tightening.

articles.title: Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
articles.expand: Pressure is building fast on EUR/USD — and two powerful macro forces could send it lower in May. Higher oil prices from Strait of Hormuz disruptions are hammering European growth… while a potentially more hawkish Fed under Kevin Warsh is supercharging the US Dollar. Bearish EUR/USD isn't just a trade on Europe — it's a broad bet on dollar strength....

articles.title: Gold declines as hawkish interest rate outlook and firm US Dollar weigh
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USDS) kicks off the week under pressure, hovering near one-month lows as hawkish interest rate expectations continue to weigh on the non-yielding metal amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,560, down nearly 1.10% on the day.

articles.title: AUD/USD Awaits Hawkish RBA to Clear 0.72 Hurdle Decisively
articles.expand: The RBA is set to hike again in the upcoming Asian session—but that’s not the real story. Markets have largely priced in a 25 basis point move to 4.35%, and the focus has already shifted to what comes next. articles.title: AUD: RBA seen leaning to another hike – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur highlights strong market conviction that the RBA will deliver a third consecutive rate hike, with futures implying a 75% probability. Inflation and expectations remain well above target and RBA rhetoric has turned more hawkish.

articles.title: Euro extends losses against US Dollar amid fears of an escalation of Iran's war
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) is retreating further from last week's highs at the 1.1785 area against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday, reaching levels below 1.1700 heading into the US session opening.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Resilient above 1.17 with ECB split – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage points out that the Euro is holding above 1.1700 even as the US Dollar (USD) firms, with markets weighing divergent European Central Bank (ECB) rhetoric. articles.title: USD/CHF rises on safe-haven USD demand while SNB outlook limits CHF downside
articles.expand: USD/CHF trades around 0.7840 on Monday, up 0.28% on the day, supported by a renewed wave of risk aversion that is boosting the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: GBP/USD drifts below 1.3550 as rising US-Iran tensions sour sentiment
articles.expand: The Pound (GBP) reverses initial gains against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday and extends its correction from Friday’s highs above 1.3650, to session lows below 1.3550.

articles.title: ECB: June leaning shapes Euro outlook – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage highlights that the European Central Bank (ECB) is now clearly leaning toward a June rate move, contrasting with the Bank of England's (BoE) preference to wait for fuller confirmation. He sees this directional guidance as a break from the prior “policy in a good place” stance.

articles.title: USD/CAD: Limited downside before Q4 – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister argues that higher Oil prices are giving the Canadian Dollar (CAD) temporary support, but warns that a fragile Canadian economy and upcoming USMCA (United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement) talks could trigger setbacks.

articles.title: ECB's Villeroy: Critical mass of data needed before tightening policy
articles.expand: In his annual letter to French President Emmanuel Macron on the state of the economy on Monday, European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker Francois Villeroy de Galhau argued that the ECB needs gather a "critical mass of data" suggesting that inflation is becoming entrenched before tightening the policy

articles.title: BoE: Expected to on prolonged hold – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists report that the Bank of England's (BoE) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) left Bank Rate at 3.75% with an 8–1 vote. Their base case is for rates to remain unchanged through 2026, though further hikes of 50–75 bps are possible if the US‑Iran conflict persists.

articles.title: ECB: Single June rate hike expected – UOB
articles.expand: UOB strategists expect the European Central Bank (ECB) to keep policy broadly steady but deliver a single 25-basis-point rate hike at the 11 Jun meeting.

articles.title: ECB's Kazimir: Policy tightening in June is all but inevitable
articles.expand: European Central Bank policymaker Peter Kazimir said on Monday that although they are not committed to any fixed interest rate path, they remain firm in their policy approach.

articles.expand: SYDNEY: Shares edged higher while oil prices flatlined in Asia on Monday, as investors drew comfort from signs of patchy progress in settling the Middle East conflict at the start of a week packed with earnings and key economic data.

articles.title: Tokyo CPI misses forecast sharply, giving BoJ room to hold despite June hike signals
articles.expand: This is a yen-negative, JGB-positive print in the near term.
